title: convolvulus sepium subsp. balticus
intro: Convolvulus sepium subsp. balticus is a subspecies of the Bindweed plant, characterized by its climbing habit and small, trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to ease digestive issues and reduce swelling. Convolvulus sepium subsp. balticus has been used to treat a range of health issues, including insomnia, anxiety, and menstrual cramps, as well as to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
